# Fixture difficulty

*Generated by Claude from the benchmark's experiment runs, and reviewed by the author.*

FULL% per fixture, **aggregated across the current model set**, by request tier — `short`
(design, one-line name), `verbose` (architectural prose), `spec` (full wiring). It maps where
the corpus is hard: a high `spec` with a near-zero `short` is a *design* gap (the model can
transcribe the wiring but not choose the topology); low across all tiers is a topology few
models build at all.

Typical runs per fixture: short n≈103, verbose n≈45, spec n≈83. Sorted by `short` FULL% (hardest design first). Model-set-dependent
— regenerate as the set grows.

## System-prompt impact on the 10 hardest (short tier)

For the hardest design fixtures, the FULL rate across system prompts — which scaffolding (if
any) unlocks the topology. Each cell is `FULL/runs` at that system prompt; **bold** = best for
that fixture. Coverage varies by system prompt, so read counts, not just rates.

| fixture | topology | topology_ports | strict_ports | conventions | formatted | best |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 074_source_follower_pmos_driving_resistor_load | 0/15 | 0/20 | 0/33 | 0/30 | 0/5 | none |
| 119_triple_cascode_opamp_single_ended_nmos | 0/15 | 0/20 | 0/33 | **1/30** | 0/5 | conventions |
| 122_super_source_follower_nmos_input | 0/15 | 0/20 | **1/33** | 0/30 | 0/5 | strict_ports |
| 034_wide_swing_cascode_pmos_mirror_1to1 | 0/15 | 0/20 | 0/33 | **2/30** | 0/5 | conventions |
| 039_wilson_mirror_4t_nmos | 0/15 | **1/20** | 0/33 | 1/30 | 0/5 | topology_ports |
| 052_folded_cascode_ota_pmos | 0/15 | **1/20** | 1/33 | 0/30 | 0/5 | topology_ports |
| 033_wide_swing_cascode_nmos_mirror_1to1 | 0/15 | 0/20 | **2/33** | 1/30 | 0/5 | strict_ports |
| 101_two_stage_diff_amp_nmos_no_cmfb_no_comp | 0/15 | **1/20** | 1/33 | 1/30 | 0/5 | topology_ports |
| 121_super_source_follower_pmos_input | 0/15 | **3/20** | 0/33 | 0/30 | 0/5 | topology_ports |
| 040_wilson_mirror_4t_pmos | 0/15 | 1/20 | 1/33 | **2/30** | 0/5 | conventions |

Reading it: a fixture that is `0/n` everywhere except one system prompt shows that scaffolding
carrying the whole result (the topology is buildable but only when the prompt resolves it); a
fixture `0/n` across *all* prompts is one no current model designs from a one-line request.
